zone_6b_notes: One of the most reliable long-blooming perennials in Zone 6b â€” blooms June through September without deadheading. Zagreb (golden yellow, compact at 18 in.) and Moonbeam (soft butter yellow, mounding) are the two classic cultivars; both are excellent in Farmington. Drought tolerant once established and virtually pest-free. Spreads slowly by rhizome; divide every 4â€“5 years when clumps die out in center. Excellent front-of-border plant. Cut back by 1/3 in midsummer if plants look tired â€” rebounds quickly for a fresh fall flush. Works beautifully alongside salvia, echinacea, and ornamental grasses.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant divisions or nursery stock in spring or fall

water: drought tolerant â€” 0.5 inch/week; poor drainage causes crown rot
